intro.title: More than a conference — *a movement.*
intro.body: Ummah Tech is a movement of Muslim technologists, founders and creatives, growing out of South Africa. Our mission is to nurture collaboration, inspire innovation and build skills among Muslims in the technology sector, for the benefit of the Ummah and humanity.

milestones.title: The first conference
milestones.body: On 23 November 2024, the first Ummah Tech Conference gathered Muslim technologists, founders and innovators in Johannesburg, with talks on empowering the Ummah and society through technology.

image.alt: A talk in progress at the first Ummah Tech Conference

milestones.title: The conference returns
milestones.body: The second edition set out to be more than an event: conversations that continue, projects that begin, and a community that keeps growing between conferences.

milestones.title: The next edition
milestones.body: The conference becomes an annual flagship, rotating between Johannesburg and Cape Town. The next edition takes place in Johannesburg on 7 November 2026.

image.alt: The Ummah Tech community gathered in Johannesburg

items.title: Big Tech displaces public institutions
items.body: Private platforms now perform roles that belonged to public bodies, funded by harvesting and monetising user data.

items.title: Algorithms shape belief and behaviour
items.body: Recommendation systems and AI decide what billions see and value, at the cost of faith, focus and social cohesion.

items.title: Our voices are censored
items.body: Throughout the war on Gaza, pro-Palestinian posts and accounts have been removed, throttled and shadowbanned. Human Rights Watch documents the suppression on Instagram and Facebook as systemic.

items.title: Dependent on providers we boycott
items.body: Google, Amazon and Microsoft sell cloud and AI services to the Israeli military, yet still host much of the Ummah's daily infrastructure.

beliefs.heading: What we stand for
beliefs.lead: These propositions shape what we build and how we build it.

items.title: _Niyyah_ (sincere intention).
items.body: Every action is driven by a pure, purposeful intention to benefit the Ummah and humanity.

items.title: Technological independence.
items.body: Striving for independence in technology to secure the Ummah's future.

items.title: Values-based technology design.
items.body: Technology is not neutral. We design and deploy it consciously, embedding Islamic values of justice, fairness and integrity in its foundations.

items.title: Rethinking progress.
items.body: Progress measured beyond GDP and consumption: justice, poverty reduction, morality and flourishing communities.

items.title: Creativity and innovation.
items.body: We encourage futuristic thinking, keeping Muslim technologists at the forefront of global tech.

join.heading: Find your people. *Build what's worth inheriting.*
join.body: If you're a Muslim technologist, founder, creative or innovator, you belong here. The WhatsApp community is where the movement lives between conferences, on the road to Ummah Tech Conference 2026 in Johannesburg.

contact.heading: Get in touch.
contact.description: Questions, speaking, volunteering or partnership. Tell us what you're reaching out about and it reaches the right person.
contact.successMessage: Thanks, we've got your message. We'll get back to you soon.
